commands.review_summary:
Adds a typed `channel_pairing_requested` plugin hook from the shared pairing challenge path, documents it, tests it, and threads account IDs through bundled channel pairing issuers.

PR surface: Source +94, Tests +165, Docs +23. Total +282 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature/API PR, not a bug report. Source inspection confirms current main lacks the new hook, while the PR currently supplies mock/unit validation rather than live hook proof.

target.title: feat: add channel pairing request hook
target.body:
Related: #68214
Related: #89569

## What Problem This Solves

Operators can already approve pending DM pairing requests, but plugin authors do
not have a small, stable hook that fires when a new channel pairing request is
created. That makes common operator workflows harder than they should be:
notify the owner, write an audit record, open a ticket, or route the request to
an existing approval surface without polling pairing state.

Issue #68214 asked for an unauthorized/pre-auth hook surface, and review notes
there called the hook direction cleaner than adding one-off suppression config.
PR #89569 explores that broader access-request workflow, but it bundles the hook
with grouped allowlists, access-request behavior, trusted hook loading, command
changes, and channel-specific pre-auth message exposure. This PR keeps the first
step intentionally smaller: observe newly created pairing requests through the
shared pairing challenge path.

This does not close every ask in #68214: it does not expose allowlist-denial
events, add suppression semantics, or replace a future `message:unauthorized` /
access-denied hook. It is the narrower pairing-request notification primitive
that can land independently and coexist with a broader access-policy hook if
maintainers want that later.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a typed plugin hook, `channel_pairing_requested`, dispatched when a
channel DM pairing request is successfully created.

The hook is deliberately observation-only and narrow:

- no config/env surface
- no allowlist migration
- no auto-approval
- no custom denial/reply workflow
- no raw inbound message body, media, or transcript exposure

The event carries only the data a trusted plugin needs for notification/audit:
channel, optional account ID, channel-scoped sender ID, pairing code, and
sender-supplied channel metadata. Hook dispatch is fire-and-forget from the
pairing flow; failures remain fail-open, and the hook has a short default
timeout so slow notification plugins are bounded independently of the requester
reply. The code is a live, single-use approval credential and should only be
delivered to trusted operator sinks; metadata is untrusted display identity text.

## User Impact

Plugin authors can now build lightweight operator-notification workflows for new
DM pairing requests without polling the pairing store or patching individual
channels.

Because the event is emitted from the shared pairing challenge helper, channels
that already use the common pairing issuer get the hook through the central
path. Existing channel call sites now pass their resolved account IDs into that
shared issuer so multi-account notifications can identify the correct account.

This does not change default channel behavior for users without a plugin hook:
pairing requests are still created the same way, existing requests are not
re-emitted, and requester replies are still sent through the existing channel
flow.

## Evidence

Focused validation:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/plugins/hooks.security.test.ts \
  src/plugins/hook-runner-global.compose.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/boundary-invariants.test.ts \
  src/pairing/pairing-challenge.test.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing.test.ts
```

Result: passed 4 Vitest shards.

Additional checks:

```bash
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/hooks.md
/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/plugins/hook-types.ts \
  src/plugins/hooks.ts \
  src/pairing/pairing-challenge.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing.ts \
  src/pairing/pairing-challenge.test.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/agent-components-dm-auth.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/dm-command-decision.ts \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor/monitor-provider.ts \
  extensions/line/src/bot-handlers.ts \
  extensions/signal/src/monitor/access-policy.ts \
  extensions/slack/src/monitor/dm-auth.ts \
  extensions/sms/src/inbound.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/dm-access.ts \
  extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.ts
/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxlint \
  src/plugins/hook-types.ts \
  src/plugins/hooks.ts \
  src/pairing/pairing-challenge.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing.ts \
  src/pairing/pairing-challenge.test.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/agent-components-dm-auth.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/dm-command-decision.ts \
  extensions/imessage/src/monitor/monitor-provider.ts \
  extensions/line/src/bot-handlers.ts \
  extensions/signal/src/monitor/access-policy.ts \
  extensions/slack/src/monitor/dm-auth.ts \
  extensions/sms/src/inbound.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/dm-access.ts \
  extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.ts
node --import tsx scripts/check-import-cycles.ts
git diff --check
```

Result: all passed.

Validation note: this worktree reused the existing dependency tree from the
neighboring OpenClaw checkout via an ignored `node_modules` symlink. No
dependency install, upgrade, or lockfile mutation was performed.

AI-assisted: implemented with Codex/OpenClaw agent assistance and reviewed
against existing hook, pairing, plugin SDK, and channel pairing call sites.
Three Claude Foreman review passes checked maintainer/API fit, security/privacy,
and implementation/test coverage before this draft was opened.
